park_name: BIG4 Dunsborough Southern Stars Holiday Park
rationale_top3: Exceptional cleanliness with spotlessly maintained ensuite-style bathrooms cleaned multiple times daily, immaculate lush green grass sites, and groundskeeping that consistently earns 'cleanest campground ever' reviews Huge spacious sites that families describe as 'massive' with room for multiple vans, creating privacy and space that sets this park apart from typical caravan parks Outstanding entertainment facilities including adventure playground with flying fox, BMX pump track, games room, weekend food trucks and coffee vans, wine tasting events and live music that keep families engaged throughout their stay
rationale_honourable: Exceptional staff service with team members guiding caravans to sites, helping with reversing, and providing warm helpful assistance that drives high repeat visitation Dog-friendly facilities with fenced sites available for booking, large off-leash exercise paddock, and pet treat bags that make this ideal for families traveling with dogs Strategic central location between Dunsborough and Busselton providing easy access to beaches, wineries and Margaret River region attractions while maintaining peaceful rural surrounds
water_fun: No pool; beaches 10-15 minutes drive away
kids_play: Adventure playground, flying fox, BMX track, games
pet_detail: Dog friendly with fenced sites available for booking, large off-leash exercise paddock, treat bags provided, dogs welcome on school holidays
best_for: Families with young children and pets seeking exceptionally maintained facilities, spacious grass sites, excellent on-site entertainment, and a premium base for exploring Dunsborough and Margaret River region
executive_summary:
PARK: BIG4 Dunsborough Southern Stars Holiday Park
SCORE: 88/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-25
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 321

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (17/20) — Reviews consistently praise the adventure playground with flying fox, BMX pump track, games room with air hockey and table tennis, bike track, food trucks on weekends, coffee vans in mornings, wine tasting events, live music, and line dancing. Families note "so much for the kids to do," "kids loved the pump track," and "barely saw the kids due to playground and bike track." Chase the Ace on Friday nights is popular. Only limitation is no pool/waterpark.

Nature (16/20) — Located in peaceful rural setting "surrounded by paddocks" with "beautiful lush rural surrounds," walking distance to Lentedal Winery through vineyard, and chickens roaming the grounds. Park features "lush green grass everywhere," "beautiful green lawns," and native wildlife including kangaroos visible from sites. Beaches are 10-15 minutes drive in Dunsborough and Busselton. Not directly on beach but excellent nature-based setting.

Cleanliness (15/15) — Exceptional scores with reviews stating "spotlessly clean," "immaculate," "pristine condition," "amenities always spotless," and "cleanest campground ever." Staff clean bathrooms multiple times daily, even "wipe the clothes line." Ensuite-style bathrooms are "spacious and huge," facilities "always clean," and "everything is so well maintained." Groundsmen constantly maintain grass sites between guests.

Value (12/15) — Reviews show mixed feedback on pricing. Multiple guests note "a bit pricey," "rather pricey compared with Perth (at least by $155 additional per week)," "on the more expensive side," and "70 dollars is impertinence for 30 square meters" (international visitor). However, many counter that "you're certainly paying for quality" and facilities justify the cost. Value score reflects premium pricing but exceptional quality.

Site Size (10/10) — Perfect score with reviews consistently praising "huge sites," "massive sites," "large well spaced caravan sites," "spacious sites," "great big sites," and "so much room on each site." Families note "could fit 3 vans in the spot," "didn't feel like we were on top of anyone," and "plenty of space between vans." Big rig sites available for large caravans.

Sentiment (10/10) — Outstanding sentiment with families stating "will definitely be back," "already booked again before we left," "booked in for another few days," and "our new favourite caravan park." Multiple reviews call it "the best caravan park in WA," "best we've stayed at in 3.5 years around Australia," and "one of the best parks in Australia." Repeat visitation extremely high.

Location (8/10) — Central location between Dunsborough (10km) and Busselton (14km) with "easy access to the Dunsborough/Margaret River region." Close to wineries, beaches, attractions, and Vasse shopping centre. Reviews note "perfect location," "central to everything," and walking distance to winery. Not directly in town or on beach, hence slight point reduction, but strategic base location praised.

WHY NOT 100:
1. No swimming pool is the most frequently mentioned limitation, with multiple families noting "NO POOL!!!!" and "could do with a pool," which is a significant amenity gap for families, especially in summer heat.
2. Strict policies including no early check-in before 1pm, no mats on grass areas, rigid cancellation policy (less than 2 weeks notice means no refunds/rescheduling), and check-in cutoff at 8pm frustrate some guests who need flexibility.
3. Limited WiFi/internet availability with reviews noting "No WiFi" and "park was too big" for coverage, which matters to families needing connectivity, though many appreciate the digital detox opportunity.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"best caravan park in WA," "immaculate lush green grass sites," "spotlessly clean amenities," "friendly and helpful staff," "huge sites so much space," "perfect for dogs fenced sites," "guided to site helped reverse," "will definitely be back"

rationale_top3: Nature & Location (30/30): The park's greatest strength is its spectacular beachfront position just 100 metres from an amazing beach with breathtaking ocean views, cliff-top walks, whale watching opportunities, and direct beach access. Walking distance to Caves House pub, cafes, and perfectly positioned for exploring Margaret River wineries and caves. Site Size & Cleanliness Issues (12/25): Major weaknesses with extremely small, cramped sites described as 'shopping centre car park' sized, narrow access roads making caravan maneuvering difficult, and consistently poor cleanliness standards including filthy bathrooms with black mould, rusty camp kitchen facilities, and overflowing bins. Poor Value for Premium Pricing (7/15): Significant criticism for overpricing with powered sites $53-$175/night and cabins $250-$423/night using dynamic pricing strategies, while facilities remain outdated and poorly maintained. Guests felt the park charges premium rates based solely on location without investing in quality facilities.
rationale_honourable: Tasman Holiday Parks - Yallingup Beach earns Bronze recognition primarily for its spectacular beachfront location and stunning natural environment. The park sits just metres from one of WA's most beautiful beaches with breathtaking ocean views, cliff-top walks, and easy access to caves, wineries, and coastal attractions. While the location is consistently praised as 'magic' and 'perfect,' the park is significantly let down by small, cramped sites, inconsistent cleanliness standards, outdated facilities, and poor value for money with pricing that doesn't match the amenity quality. Families visiting should expect a beautiful coastal setting but be prepared for tight accommodation quarters and variable maintenance standards.
water_fun: No pool facilities mentioned in reviews
kids_play: Playground across road; minimal on-site entertainment
pet_detail: Dogs allowed on all sites and in 5 dog-friendly cabins, must be kept on leash at all times
best_for: This park best suits families with smaller rigs or tent campers who prioritize absolute beachfront location and are willing to trade spacious sites and modern facilities for direct beach access and stunning coastal views
executive_summary:
PARK: Tasman Holiday Parks - Yallingup Beach
SCORE: 62/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-06-16
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 216

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (6/20) — Reviews consistently note the park has "nothing for the kids except grass area to run and play" with no pool, waterpark, or organized activities. A playground exists "across the road" from the park. The games room had vandalism issues and some families were disappointed kids disappeared to a TV room. No bounce pillows or structured entertainment mentioned except occasional food trucks.

Nature (18/20) — The park's standout feature with "spectacular" beachfront location just "100 metres to an amazing beach" with "breathtaking ocean views." Guests praised "stunning coastal breeze," cliff-top walks, caves nearby, and "one of the most beautiful spots in Yallingup." Direct beach access, whale watching opportunities, and natural bushland surroundings earned high marks.

Cleanliness (8/15) — Major inconsistency emerged across 216 reviews. Multiple guests reported bathrooms "absolutely filthy," "never cleaned," with "stains on toilets," "black mould," "soap scum everywhere," and facilities "dirty regardless what time I went." Camp kitchen described as "rusty," "tiny and grotty," "health hazard." Some noted facilities were clean during their stay, but negative cleanliness mentions far outweighed positive.

Value (7/15) — Overwhelming criticism of pricing with guests calling it "overpriced," "rip-off," and "extreme." Powered sites ranged $53-$175/night, cabins $250-$423/night. Many noted "doesn't justify" the cost given small sites and poor facilities. "Dynamic pricing" criticized. Guests felt location was "taking your money because it can" without matching facility quality.

Site Size (4/10) — Consistent complaints about "very small and super tight" sites, "cramped," "tiny camp spaces." Caravans had "most difficult time getting into spot," roads "not wide enough for cars to pass," sites "so close" neighbors felt like they were sharing space. Multiple mentions of difficulty maneuvering rigs, "couldn't put awning out," and "shopping centre car park" sized sites.

Sentiment (7/10) — Mixed sentiment overall. Many long-term guests noted decline: "been coming 40 years, so disappointed," "gone downhill since Tasman took over." Some would "never back again," others "100% stay here again." Positive mentions of helpful staff (especially Mary) balanced against complaints of "rude" service. Location loyalty conflicted with facility disappointment.

Location (12/10) — Near-perfect location score with park "perfectly located opposite the beach," "walk straight over road to amazing beach," close to Caves House pub, general store, bakery, and cafe all "walking distance." Central to Margaret River region (30 min to Busselton, close to wineries and caves). "Can't beat the location" appeared repeatedly despite other criticisms.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Sites are extremely small and cramped with very narrow access roads making caravan entry/exit difficult, with guests literally feeling like they're "sharing awnings" with neighbors and unable to open awnings or place chairs comfortably.
2. Cleanliness standards are inconsistent and often poor with multiple reports of dirty bathrooms never properly cleaned, rusty camp kitchen facilities, black mould, overflowing bins, and "filthy" amenities that don't reflect the premium pricing charged.
3. Value for money is extremely poor with dynamic pricing reaching $175/night for grass sites and $423 for tiny cabins, while facilities remain outdated, cramped, and poorly maintained - the park is "taking your money because it can" based on location alone without investing in proper facilities.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"location is absolutely stunning right by the beach," "sites are so small and super tight squeezing van in," "bathrooms absolutely filthy never cleaned," "overpriced at $201 per night for caravan site," "camp kitchen like a tin shed with rust," "100 metres to an amazing beach," "cramped, smelly and cold showers," "beautiful location but facilities let it down"

rationale_top3: Location (10/10): Universally praised as the park's standout feature with direct beach access, walking distance to Caves House pub, bakery, and general store, plus central access to Margaret River region attractions. Reviewers consistently describe it as 'absolutely stunning' and 'you simply can't beat the location.' Nature (18/20): Near-perfect scores for spectacular beachfront setting with clifftop walks, whale watching from sites, breathtaking coastal views, and proximity to caves. The natural surroundings are described as 'magic' and 'surrounded by nature.' Cleanliness (6/15): Major weakness with extensive complaints of 'absolutely filthy' facilities, bathrooms not cleaned during 4-7 day stays, mould in cabins, and facilities 'desperately in need of a deep clean.' This significantly impacts the overall score.
rationale_honourable: Site Size (4/10) and Value (7/15) are critical weaknesses. Sites are consistently described as 'very small,' 'super tight,' and 'cramped' with difficult maneuverability for caravans 17ft+. Guests feel the park is 'overpriced' at $100-$200+ per night given the facility condition, with many stating it's 'taking your money because it can' based on location alone. Entertainment (4/20) is minimal with 'nothing for the kids' on-site except grass areas, and the playground is located across the main road outside the park.
water_fun: No pool; beach access across road
kids_play: Playground across road; minimal on-site entertainment
pet_detail: Dogs allowed on all sites and in select cabins (5 dog-friendly cabins available); must be kept on leash at all times.
best_for: Families with self-contained setups who value spectacular beach access and coastal scenery over modern amenities and spacious sites.
executive_summary:
PARK: Tasman Holiday Parks - Yallingup Caves
SCORE: 58/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-24
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 216

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (4/20) — Reviews consistently note "nothing for the kids" on-site except grass areas to run and play. Multiple families mentioned a TV room and games room, but parents expressed frustration that children disappeared to screens rather than outdoor play. A playground exists but is located across the main road, not within the park itself. No jumping pillow, pool, or organized activities mentioned.

Nature (18/20) — The park's strongest asset with near-universal praise for its "absolutely stunning" beachfront location. Reviewers highlight direct beach access "just across the road," spectacular coastal views, clifftop walks, proximity to caves, and "breathtaking" natural surroundings. Multiple mentions of whale watching from sites, beautiful sunsets over the ocean, and being "surrounded by nature."

Cleanliness (6/15) — This is a major weakness with extensive complaints across both recent and older reviews. Common phrases include "absolutely filthy," "never cleaned," "stains on toilets still there when I left," "disgusting floors," "dirty marks on walls," and "desperately in need of a deep clean." Multiple guests reported bathrooms not cleaned during 4-7 day stays. Cabins received complaints about mould, food stains, dirty floors, and poor maintenance.

Value (7/15) — Strong negative sentiment about pricing with frequent mentions of being "overpriced," "rip-off," and not worth $100-$201 per night for powered sites or $250-$423 for cabins given the facility condition. Many reviewers felt the park was "taking your money because it can" due to location alone. Dynamic pricing complaints emerged with rates doubling. Some acknowledged fair pricing in off-season but overwhelmingly guests felt value didn't match cost.

Site Size (4/10) — Widespread complaints that sites are "very small," "super tight," "cramped," and "difficult to navigate." Multiple reviewers with 17-21ft caravans reported extreme difficulty maneuvering into sites with narrow one-way roads. Phrases like "may as well have shared our awning" and "everyone is on top of each other" appear frequently. Some sites described as "the size of a shopping centre car park."

Sentiment (7/10) — Mixed overall sentiment with a clear divide. Long-term returners (10-40+ years) expressed disappointment that standards have declined, particularly since Tasman takeover. However, many first-time visitors loved the location enough to plan return trips. Positive mentions of friendly staff (especially Mary) balanced by complaints of rude or dismissive service. Repeat visitation appears driven entirely by location rather than facilities.

Location (10/10) — Universally praised as the park's standout feature. "Perfect location," "you simply can't beat the location," "hands down the nicest place." Walking distance to beach, Caves House pub, excellent bakery, general store, and surf breaks. Central to Margaret River region wineries, Busselton, and Dunsborough. Direct access to Cape to Cape walking track. Location alone keeps this park fully booked despite facility issues.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Cleanliness is severely substandard with bathrooms, toilets, and cabins consistently described as dirty, unhygienic, and inadequately maintained, with multiple reports of facilities not being cleaned for entire 4-7 day stays despite premium pricing.

2. Sites are far too small and tightly packed for modern caravans and RVs, with narrow access roads causing significant maneuverability problems and guests feeling "cramped" with no privacy from neighbors literally meters away.

3. Facilities are dated and inadequate for the number of guests, with tiny camp kitchens described as "pokey sheds," insufficient toilet/shower blocks for 90+ sites, rusty equipment, and amenities that "need desperate renovation" yet don't reflect the $100-$200+ nightly pricing.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"location is absolutely stunning right by the beach," "facilities are below average," "bathroom wasn't cleaned until day 3," "sites are so small," "very cramped in," "overpriced for what you get," "camp kitchen is tiny," "dirty facilities," "spectacular location," "simply taking your money because it can"

rationale_top3: Nature & Location (16/20): The park's strongest asset is its stunning beachfront position with beautiful coastal views and direct beach access, though accessibility challenges exist including rocky terrain and difficult pram access. Entertainment & Facilities (10/20): Significant shortcomings with entertainment venues described as extremely small and inadequate for park size, outdated playground equipment, and limited activities that disappointed families expecting comprehensive facilities. Cleanliness & Maintenance (7/15): Major systematic cleaning failures dominate reviews with reports of filthy caravans, stained furnishings, greasy kitchens, blood-stained bedding, and widespread dog mess issues, though some chalets received praise for being immaculate.
rationale_honourable: Sandy Bay Holiday Park earns Bronze classification primarily for its stunning beachfront location and excellent natural setting. While the park faces significant challenges with cleanliness standards, maintenance issues, and inconsistent service delivery, the immediate beach access and beautiful coastal environment remain genuine strengths. Families who prioritize location over facilities and are prepared for mixed service standards may find value here as a base for exploring Northumberland's coastline.
water_fun: Small indoor heated pool, limited capacity
kids_play: Small entertainment venue, playground equipment dated rusty
pet_detail: Dog friendly accommodation available, £98 per week for two dogs mentioned, beach access for dogs nearby
best_for: Sandy Bay suits budget-conscious families who want direct beach access and can overlook facility limitations, or those using the park purely as a base for exploring Northumberland's coastline and attractions.
executive_summary:
PARK: Sandy Bay Holiday Park
SCORE: 52/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-23
REWIEWS ANALYSED: 338

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (10/20) — Reviews reveal major concerns: entertainment venue described as "extremely small" and "inadequate for size of site", soft play removed without website updates disappointing families, kids playground equipment "old tired rusty with broken fencing", arcade "tiny good for adults bad for kids", limited activities with some overpriced (£8 pumpkin carving), though some positive mentions of enthusiastic entertainment staff like Chantel and Erin.

Nature (16/20) — Strongest aspect with consistently praised "stunning beach", "beautiful beachfront location", "stone's throw from beach", "amazing sea views", though beach access "partially across rocks dodgy for very young or old", "near impossible with prams", significant dog mess issues noted, overall "beautiful coastal environment" despite access challenges.

Cleanliness (7/15) — Major critical theme: caravans "absolutely disgusting", "floors definitely not been cleaned", "carpets sofa heavily stained", "greasy kitchen", "blood stains on duvet", "dog mess all over site", "cleaning standards horrendous", showbar "filthy", some positive mentions of chalets being "immaculate" but overwhelming evidence of inconsistent cleaning standards.

Value (6/15) — Significant concerns: drinks "seriously overpriced" (£6.50 pint), food "very expensive" (£20 spaghetti), "3x price of Costa del Sol", many felt "didn't receive what paid for", banding/grading issues (paid for upgrades, received same units), though some found "great value for money" especially chalets, mixed perception overall.

Site Size (6/10) — Reviews consistently note "site not massive", "very small", "facilities far too small for size of park", "cramped", swimming pool "very small", "only 6 changing rooms", lack of parking spaces near caravans forcing muddy walks, though some appreciated compact nature as "easy to walk to" facilities.

Sentiment (4/10) — Deeply polarized: numerous "worst holiday ever" declarations, "never coming back", "will not book Parkdean again", accounts of 10-15 year regulars stating "not same park we grew to love", safety concerns about youths/antisocial behavior, countered by some loyal returners praising location and staff, but negative sentiment dominates recent reviews.

Location (3/10) — Appears to be TWO different parks conflated: Dunsborough WA Australia location with consistent 4-5 star reviews praising beach access and cleanliness, versus Northumberland UK Parkdean location with predominantly negative reviews, scoring reflects UK site which dominates review volume with poor accessibility, muddy conditions, and distant from major attractions (40 miles to some).

WHY NOT 100:
Cleanliness crisis: Overwhelming evidence of systematic cleaning failures including filthy caravans with previous guests' items, blood-stained bedding, grease, mould, dog feces throughout site, and management dismissiveness when issues reported. Entertainment deficit: Facilities described as inadequate for park size with small cramped venues, removed features still advertised, dated broken playground equipment, limited programming, and inconsistent food service hours disappointing families. Service and safety breakdown: Multiple accounts of rude dismissive staff, unresolved maintenance issues, antisocial behavior including youth gangs in balaclavas, power outages poorly handled, and apparent deterioration from previous standards with long-term customers reporting park "not same we grew to love".

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"absolutely immaculate clean chalet", "worst holiday I have ever been on", "fantastic location right next to beach", "caravan absolutely disgusting", "staff blatantly rude dismissive", "site overrun with youths balaclavas", "cleaning standards horrendous", "beautiful beach walking distance"

rationale_top3: Severe cleanliness and maintenance failures with filthy accommodation, broken facilities, mould, unusable kitchen equipment, and overwhelmingly negative hygiene reports across bathrooms, cabins, and shared facilities. Extremely cramped sites with no privacy, wedged between permanent residents and right next to a busy highway causing constant noise disturbance day and night. Run-down entertainment facilities that are either broken, closed, or described as 'ghost town' condition, with management appearing to have given up on reinvestment despite increasing prices.
rationale_honourable: Location between Busselton and Dunsborough provides convenient beach access just 200 metres away, and the park welcomes dogs with dedicated wash facilities.
water_fun: Small pool available, often crowded with kids
kids_play: Jumping pillow popular, mini golf very run down
pet_detail: Dog friendly with dog wash facility available; dogs must be on leads at all times
best_for: Families travelling with dogs who prioritise location and affordability over modern facilities and are comfortable with basic, dated accommodation.
executive_summary:
PARK: Fourseasons Holiday Park
SCORE: 38/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-20
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 81

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Reviews mention a jumping pillow that kids enjoy, small pool, mini golf that's described as 'ghost town' condition and 'hasn't been touched for years', basketball ring broken, recreation hall closed and 'nothing in it for kids', playground equipment 'full of cob webbs'. Families note 'only thing kids had to do was bounce on the bouncing pillow'.

Nature (12/20) — Park features 'lovely lawns & beautiful trees', 'wonderful canopy of peppermint trees', 'shaded sites nestled amongst trees'. Beach access is '200 metres away' across the road, dog beach nearby. However, sites are 'right next to a busy road' with 'heavy traffic day and night', and park has 'abandoned trailers and buildings scattered around the edge'.

Cleanliness (3/15) — Overwhelmingly negative feedback: 'extremely run down', 'absolutely filthy and greasy' pots and pans, 'dirty bathrooms despite claims cleaned twice daily', 'toilets stink and really need a proper clean', 'film of dirt on all surfaces', 'mould in bathrooms', 'poop in all toilets', 'floors haven't seen vacuum or mop in a long time', 'bins so dirty you don't want to touch them'. Multiple reviews describe it as 'most uncleaned accommodation we've ever stayed at'.

Value (5/15) — Mixed but trending negative: described as 'extremely good value' and 'affordable' by some, but many cite '$45 a night for unpowered - what a joke', 'quite pricey for the conditions', prices 'nearly double' what it used to cost, '$500 cleaning fee whether unit is clean or not'. Several note 'an airbnb would've been cheaper'. Value perception severely impacted by poor condition.

Site Size (3/10) — Consistently poor: 'sites very tight, hard to get into', 'powered site was unfortunately quite tiny', 'extremely tight sites', 'could almost reach out the window and touch camper tent beside our van', 'no privacy whatsoever', 'very limited parking space', sites 'wedged in between permanent residents'. Families struggle with space for caravans and setups.

Sentiment (3/10) — Predominantly negative with strong warnings: 'would not recommend', 'never stay here', 'don't waste your money', 'will never book here again', 'couldn't wait to leave', 'save people from getting disappointed'. Long-term guests report 'standards have gone downhill badly over 10 years'. Small number of positive repeat visitors, mostly dog owners who value the location despite issues.

Location (4/10) — Best feature cited: 'excellent location' between Busselton and Dunsborough, 'beach right across the road', 'perfect hub to explore surrounding areas'. However, major negative is 'sites right next to busy road', 'very noisy even at night', 'trucks and cars traffic day and night'. Location advantage significantly undermined by road noise.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Severe cleanliness and maintenance failures with filthy accommodation, broken facilities, mould, unusable kitchen equipment, and overwhelmingly negative hygiene reports across bathrooms, cabins, and shared facilities.
2. Extremely cramped sites with no privacy, wedged between permanent residents and right next to a busy highway causing constant noise disturbance day and night.
3. Run-down entertainment facilities that are either broken, closed, or described as 'ghost town' condition, with management appearing to have given up on reinvestment despite increasing prices.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"extremely run down trailer park", "facilities are very poor", "absolutely filthy and greasy", "most uncleaned accommodation we've ever stayed at", "dirty bathrooms despite claims cleaned twice daily", "sites very tight, hard to get into", "too many permanent residents who think they own the place", "couldn't wait to leave", "jumping pillow was the only thing kids had to do", "beach 200 metres away", "dog friendly with dog wash facility", "run down and they should not be renting some cabins"
